mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of triggered instances or the t-statistic for the return difference is insignificant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative 5-day forward returns in >50% of cases during early-cycle periods across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation rate exceeding 0.4% forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months are followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances during tightening cycles, invalidating the discount rate channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows or predicts negative returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across rolling 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5% signals a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening economic momentum, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes are followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations during late-cycle periods, breaking the momentum link.
